deep_dive.title: Experience Memory Graph: One-Shot Error Correction for Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) agents have shown remarkable capabilities in autonomous decision-making by generating sequential trajectories of states, actions, and observations. However, in complex, long-horizon tasks, these agents frequently suffer from compounding errors and struggle to recover from failures. Existing self-correction mechanisms rely on prompt-based reflection, which is inherently brittle, incurs heavy time and API costs due to iterative trial-and-error loops, and produces task-specific memory that may be hard to generalize to new scenarios. To address this, we propose Experience Memory Graph (EMG), a framework that reformulates agent failure recovery as a graph matching problem. At training time, we convert both failed exploration trajectories and successful expert trajectories into directed action decision graphs. By matching these graphs, we extract common subgraphs (successful workflows) and graph edit paths that explicitly indicate how to correct failures (e.g., which actions to add, delete, or relabel under a given observation), and store them in a memory graph with intra-task nodes and cross-task edges. At test time, EMG retrieves relevant insights and guides the agent in a single, loop-free execution. Experiments on ALFWorld and ScienceWorld show that EMG consistently outperforms state-of-the-art reflection baselines in success rate and average reward, while requiring no test-time trial-and-error.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Framework for agent error correction via graph matching of past experiences, directly addressing autonomous agent post-training improvement.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过图匹配利用历史经验的智能体错误纠正框架，直接提升自主智能体后训练表现。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过图匹配利用历史经验的智能体错误纠正框架，直接提升自主智能体后训练表现。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过图匹配利用历史经验的智能体错误纠正框架，直接提升自主智能体后训练表现。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes EMG, a framework that converts agent trajectories into a graph and uses one-shot correction by matching failures to successful experiences, reducing costly trial-and-error loops.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对LLM智能体在长周期任务中易累积错误且自纠正成本高的问题，本文提出经验记忆图谱（EMG）框架，将失败和成功的探索轨迹转换为图结构，通过图匹配实现一次性错误纠正。实验表明，EMG显著提升了智能体的任务成功率，并减少了迭代试错开销，为智能体后训练提供了一种高效的错误恢复方法。

deep_dive.title: Reward-Free Evolving Agents via Pairwise Validator
deep_dive.abstract: A self-evolving agentic loop repeatedly proposes a tweaked version of an agent (its prompt template or program) and accepts or rejects the change based on a per-iteration quality signal. Designing that signal is often the costly part of the project: a reliable scalar reward requires domain expertise and labeled examples that are themselves as expensive to assemble as the agent's underlying task. We propose replacing the scalar at the accept/reject gate with a pairwise validator: a frozen LLM that, given the parent and child candidate, returns a binary verdict on which is better. Pairwise judgment is generally easier and more stable than absolute scoring, due to its contrastive nature, which mitigates the need for strict scale calibration. The validator also requires no training of its own. We integrate the validator into three published self-evolving engines (GEPA, ADRS, ShinkaEvolve) and report two flavors: Adaptive Focus, which retains the engine's existing val-set parent selection, and Soft Elo, which lets the validator's verdicts drive parent selection so that val-set rewards drop as well. Across multiple agents and two artifact substrates (prompt and code), our method matches or exceeds the full-reward baseline on the majority of settings we evaluate, and the pattern survives a cross-family validator swap. The pairwise gate is thus a drop-in replacement for per-step reward design at competitive task accuracy without the labeling cost.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Self-evolving agents without scalar rewards, using pairwise validation, a direct technique for autonomous agent post-training.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 无需标量奖励的自进化智能体，使用成对验证，是自主智能体后训练的直接技术。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 无需标量奖励的自进化智能体，使用成对验证，是自主智能体后训练的直接技术。

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes replacing scalar rewards with a frozen LLM pairwise validator for self-evolving agents, reducing the need for expensive domain-specific labeling and enabling iterative agent improvement.

deep_dive.title: PFAdapter: Hierarchical LoRA Decomposition for Personalized Federated MLLMs
deep_dive.abstract: Agentic AI systems are reshaping communications and networking by deploying autonomous intelligent agents capable of collaborative learning while maintaining data privacy at network edges. Within distributed network environments, Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) serve as cognitive engines for edge devices, yet federated fine-tuning faces substantial challenges in balancing global knowledge aggregation with local adaptation under heterogeneous network conditions. Conventional federated protocols typically rely on uniform parameter aggregation, which conflates domain-invariant features with client-specific nuances, thereby resulting in suboptimal personalization and excessive communication overhead. To address these challenges, we propose PFAdapter, a communication-efficient framework introducing hierarchical LoRA decomposition to explicitly separate adapter parameters into global-shared and local-private components. Query and key projections are assigned to global synchronization for capturing universal multimodal semantics across the network, while value and output projections remain localized for edge-specific adaptation. Additionally, orthogonality regularization based on the Frobenius norm enforces strict separation between these components, preventing redundant feature learning. Selective aggregation protocols synchronize only global-shared components across the federated network, preserving local expertise and reducing communication costs by nearly 50%. Extensive experiments on VQA-RAD, SLAKE, Hateful Memes, and CrisisMMD datasets demonstrate that PFAdapter consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, achieving accuracy improvements ranging from 2.4% to 4.8% across diverse edge intelligence tasks. Consequently, our framework establishes an efficient solution for agentic AI deployment in resource-constrained communication networks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes hierarchical LoRA decomposition for federated fine-tuning of MLLMs in agentic AI systems
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出针对智能体AI系统中多模态大模型的分层LoRA分解方法进行联邦微调
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出针对智能体AI系统中多模态大模型的分层LoRA分解方法进行联邦微调
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出针对智能体AI系统中多模态大模型的分层LoRA分解方法进行联邦微调
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PFAdapter proposes hierarchical LoRA decomposition for personalized federated fine-tuning of MLLMs in agentic AI systems, addressing the balance between global knowledge and local adaptation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对分布式环境中多模态大模型智能体联邦微调的全局-本地适应矛盾，本文提出PFAdapter，采用分层LoRA分解解耦共享与个性化参数。实验表明，该方法在多个多模态任务上提升了个性化性能并降低通信开销，为智能体协同学习提供了高效的后训练方案。

deep_dive.title: Multi-Head Latent Control: A Unified Interface for LLM Agent Decision Making
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models are increasingly deployed as agents, but reliable agentic behavior requires more than next-token prediction. At inference time, it is preferred that an agent can decide whether to proceed with its current reasoning, defer to a stronger model, request additional information, invoke external tools, or abstain under the given setup. Existing approaches address these decisions through prompt-level routing, external orchestration, or task-specific fine-tuning, which primarily rely on input-side signals, and are often costly and difficult to maintain as model backbones evolve. We ask whether such control decisions can be inferred directly from a model's latent generation process. We introduce Multi-Head Latent Control, a lightweight layer that reads hidden-state trajectories from a frozen LLM or VLM to produce deployment-time control signals. A Capability Head predicts whether the current model can solve the instance or should defer to a stronger collaborator, while a Resolution Head predicts appropriate resolution decision Clarification, Tool Use, Abstention, or Direct Answering. Both heads are trained only on latent traces from the same frozen LLM backbone, enabling post hoc adaptation without modifying the model. Across language and vision-language settings, Multi-Head Latent Control consistently improves the quality-cost tradeoff of multi-model systems, enabling early handoff from partial generations and more accurate intervention decisions. In routed execution (small + large model), it reduces large-model usage by up to 90.7 percent on AndroidWorld and 27-53 percent on average across benchmarks, while retaining most of large-model performance. Additionally, the learned control signals improve tool-use decision quality, yielding up to +158 percent relative score gain and 65.5 percent fewer missed-required tool calls.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes training lightweight control heads on LLMs for agent decision-making, directly relevant to post-training of autonomous agents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出在LLM上训练轻量控制头用于智能体决策，直接关联自主智能体后训练。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出在LLM上训练轻量控制头用于智能体决策，直接关联自主智能体后训练。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出在LLM上训练轻量控制头用于智能体决策，直接关联自主智能体后训练。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Multi-Head Latent Control, a lightweight method that infers agent control decisions from latent states during generation, enabling inference-time behaviors like proceeding, deferring, or abstaining without extensive retraining.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型智能体在推理时缺乏可靠自主决策能力的问题，提出多头潜在控制方法，从模型潜在状态中推断控制决策，支持继续、延迟、请求信息或弃权等行为。该方法轻量且不依赖输入侧信号，实验验证了其在提升智能体自主决策稳定性方面的有效性，为智能体后训练提供了新的实用途径。

quick_skim.title: Non-vacuous Generalization Bounds for Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards
quick_skim.abstract: While reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) is widely used to improve the reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs), the generalizability of the resulting models remains poorly understood. In this work, we establish the first non-vacuous generalization bounds for parameter-efficient RLVR fine-tuning at the billion-parameter scale. Our approach adapts PAC-Bayes compression bounds to this setting, and addresses the inherent stochasticity of token generation by applying the Gumbel-max reparameterization trick. To operationalize these bounds, we propose the Progressive RLVR framework, which integrates RLVR with on-policy distillation, TinyLoRA, and model quantization. Progressive RLVR empirically retains 84-97% performance of standard LoRA fine-tuning while producing models that are 14,796x more compressible. We show that this framework yields non-vacuous generalization bounds in four domains: mathematical problem-solving, programming, general-knowledge reasoning, and Text-to-SQL. Our bounds exceed the accuracy of the base model by 9-51% and lie within 6-11% of the accuracy of the fine-tuned models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: RLVR fine-tuning for LLMs, proposes Progressive RLVR framework for post-training generalization.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对LLM的RLVR微调，提出Progressive RLVR框架改善后训练泛化。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对LLM的RLVR微调，提出Progressive RLVR框架改善后训练泛化。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对LLM的RLVR微调，提出Progressive RLVR框架改善后训练泛化。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Establishes non-vacuous generalization bounds for RLVR fine-tuning of billion-parameter LLMs via PAC-Bayes compression and Gumbel-max trick, proposing Progressive RLVR that retains 84-97% performance.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对强化学习可验证奖励（RLVR）微调大语言模型泛化性不明的问题，本文利用PAC-Bayes压缩界和Gumbel-max重参数化，首次为十亿参数级模型建立了非空泛化边界。提出渐进式RLVR框架，结合在线策略蒸馏、TinyLoRA和量化，在实验中保留了84-97%的性能，为RLVR后训练提供了理论保证与实用方案。

quick_skim.title: From Critic to Confidence: PPO for Language-Based Quantitative Prediction with Confidence Estimation
quick_skim.abstract: LLMs can perform language-based quantitative prediction from unstructured inputs, but remain susceptible to hallucinations and overconfident errors, making it critical to know not only what a model predicts, but when its predictions can be trusted. We introduce CARE-PPO, a reinforcement learning framework that establishes a connection between loss prediction for uncertainty estimation and actor-critic PPO fine-tuning, enabling joint learning of accurate numerical estimates and reliable confidence signals in language-based quantitative prediction. CARE-PPO uses a Confidence-Aligned Reward for Estimation, defined as a function of prediction error, to provide dense error-aware feedback to the actor while inducing the critic to learn a value function aligned with prediction quality. During inference, we repurpose the critic as a confidence estimator. Across two real-world tasks in healthcare and finance and two Qwen-3 model scales (4B and 8B), CARE-PPO achieves strong quantitative prediction performance, while producing significantly better-aligned confidence estimates through the critic than logit-based and verbalized baselines. These gains persist under realistic out-of-distribution settings across domains, spanning linguistic and domain shifts. Finally, CARE-PPO reduces task-specific overfitting on general instruction-following prompts, consistent with the broader generalization advantages of RL fine-tuning over supervised approaches.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Uses PPO-based RL fine-tuning for quantitative prediction with confidence, a reinforcement learning post-training method potentially applicable to financial agents.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用基于PPO的强化学习微调进行带置信度的定量预测，是一种可应用于金融智能体的强化学习后训练方法。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用基于PPO的强化学习微调进行带置信度的定量预测，是一种可应用于金融智能体的强化学习后训练方法。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用基于PPO的强化学习微调进行带置信度的定量预测，是一种可应用于金融智能体的强化学习后训练方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CARE-PPO, a reinforcement learning framework that jointly trains LLMs for accurate numerical prediction and reliable confidence estimation via a confidence-aligned reward and PPO fine-tuning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型在定量预测中易产生幻觉与过度自信的问题，提出CARE-PPO框架，将预测误差转化为置信度对齐奖励，通过PPO微调同时优化预测准确度和置信度估计。在多个预测任务上，该方法显著提升了模型输出的可靠性，为金融等需要可信数值预测的领域提供了可借鉴的强化学习后训练技术。

quick_skim.title: Leveraging Instruction Tuning and Merging for Reasoning Model Adaptation
quick_skim.abstract: Reasoning language models (RLMs) have demonstrated impressive performance in domains such as mathematics and coding. These domains permit reliable verification of model outputs, which is important for enabling the reinforcement learning that drives RLM performance gains. However, training RLMs on domains that lack reliable verifiers remains challenging. Meanwhile, for both verifiable and unverifiable domains, large amounts of unused supervised fine-tuning data with human-written solutions exist. In this work, we show that these data can be used efficiently to further improve RLM performance. For this, we first use classic instruction tuning, supervised fine-tuning without reasoning traces, on the RLM. Next, we merge our instruction-tuned model with the original reasoning model, recovering its reasoning behavior on the target domain. Our extensive evaluation demonstrates that our technique improves RLM performance in both verifiable and hard-to-verify domains, including coding and text summarization, while preserving RLM capabilities across other domains. Importantly, our method is highly cost-effective, enabling such improvements for less than USD $3.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Instruction tuning and model merging adapt reasoning models, a post-training technique applicable to financial task adaptation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 指令微调与模型合并是适用于金融任务适配的大模型后训练技术
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 指令微调与模型合并是适用于金融任务适配的大模型后训练技术
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 指令微调与模型合并是适用于金融任务适配的大模型后训练技术
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper shows that instruction tuning without reasoning traces followed by model merging can efficiently adapt reasoning LLMs to domains lacking verifiers, leveraging existing supervised data.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对推理大模型在无可靠验证器的领域难以训练的问题，本文先用指令微调（无推理轨迹的有监督微调）适配模型，再与原始模型合并。实验表明该方法能有效利用现有人工解答数据，提升模型在不可验证领域的性能。这为将推理模型扩展到金融等缺乏自动验证的领域提供了实用的后训练路径。

quick_skim.title: Supervised Fine-Tuning vs. In-Context Learning: An Equilibrium Analysis of LLM Personalization under Congestion
quick_skim.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have revolutionized AI services, but a critical tension emerges: while personalization improves model performance, it consumes scarce computational resources that users must share. When should a user invest in expensive Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) versus lightweight In-Context Learning (ICL)? How does congestion from other users' personalization choices reshape these incentives? And what strategies should platforms adopt when offering multiple personalization algorithms?   We develop a tractable framework for LLM serving that captures the statistical-economic trade-offs users face. Our analysis yields several surprising insights. First, we show that ICL and SFT dominate in different regimes, determined by an interplay between pretraining coverage and data signal-to-noise ratios, but congestion can flip these rankings. Second, equilibrium resource consumption exhibits pronounced non-monotonicity: improving pretraining precision reduces the congestion, while broader pretraining coverage and harder tasks sometimes increase it. Third, we prove that offering both personalization methods never hurts the platform's maximal profits, despite potentially increasing computational load.   Experiments with GPT-2 on linear regression tasks validate our theoretical predictions about algorithm performance. Complementing these results, our review of documentation from 21 major AI platforms shows that the share offering both SFT and ICL increased from 9.5% in 2021 to 71.4% in 2025, consistent with our platform-design implications.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Analyzes supervised fine-tuning versus in-context learning for LLM personalization, relevant to fine-tuning methods for financial model agents.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 分析LLM个性化中监督微调与上下文学习的权衡，与金融大模型智能体微调方法相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 分析LLM个性化中监督微调与上下文学习的权衡，与金融大模型智能体微调方法相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 分析LLM个性化中监督微调与上下文学习的权衡，与金融大模型智能体微调方法相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This equilibrium analysis shows when SFT or ICL dominates for LLM personalization under congestion, offering insights for cost-effective adaptation strategies.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大模型个性化计算资源紧张的问题，本文构建统计-经济框架分析监督微调与上下文学习的均衡。发现上下文学习在基模型质量低、拥塞轻时占优，而微调在基模型强、拥塞重时更优。该研究为金融智能体的个性化后训练方案选择提供了理论指导。
